Class Section

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.block.Block<Map<Object,Block<?>>>
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.block.implementation.Section
Direct Known Subclasses:
YamlDocument
public class Section extends Block<Map<Object,Block<?>>>
Represents one YAML section (map), while storing its contents and comments. Section can also be referred to as collection of mapping entries (key=value pairs).

BoostedYAML represents every entry (key=value pair) in the file as a block, which also applies to sections - sections actually store blocks, which carry the raw value - Java object. Please learn more about implementations at wiki.

ADDITIONAL METHOD DOCUMENTATION:

  1. Or value from defaults (#1)

    If this section has an equivalent in the defaults (hasDefaults()), instead of returning that value, returns the result of invoking the same method on the default section getDefaults().

    This behaviour can be disabled (#3).

  2. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2)

    If there is no value at the given route in this section and this section has an equivalent in the defaults (hasDefaults()), compares value at the same route in the default section getDefaults(). If no value is found there, immediately returns false.

    This behaviour can be disabled (#3).

  3. Disable use of defaults (#3)

    You can disable use of the defaults (if any) by the method using GeneralSettings.Builder.setUseDefaults(boolean).

    Constructor
    Description
     
    Section(YamlDocument root, Section parent, Route route, Block<?> previous, Map<?,?> mappings)
    Creates a section using the given relatives, previous block and mappings.
     
    Section(YamlDocument root, Section parent, Route route, org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.Node keyNode, org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.MappingNode valueNode, ExtendedConstructor constructor)
    Creates a section using the given relatives, nodes and constructor, which is used to retrieve the actual Java representations of nodes (and sub-nodes).
    protected
    Section(Map<Object,Block<?>> defaultMap)
    Creates a section using the given (not necessarily empty) instance of default map.

  • Constructor Details

    • Section

      public Section(@NotNull YamlDocument root, @Nullable Section parent, @NotNull Route route, @Nullable org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.Node keyNode, @NotNull org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.MappingNode valueNode, @NotNull ExtendedConstructor constructor)
      Creates a section using the given relatives, nodes and constructor, which is used to retrieve the actual Java representations of nodes (and sub-nodes).
      Parameters:
      root - root file
      parent - parent section (or null if this is the root section)
      route - absolute (starting from the root file) route to this section
      keyNode - node which represents the key to this section, used only to retrieve comments
      valueNode - node which represents this section's contents
      constructor - constructor used to construct all the nodes contained within the root file, used to retrieve Java instances of the nodes
      See Also:
      • superclass constructor used

    • Section

      public Section(@NotNull YamlDocument root, @Nullable Section parent, @NotNull Route route, @Nullable Block<?> previous, @NotNull Map<?,?> mappings)
      Creates a section using the given relatives, previous block and mappings.
      Parameters:
      root - root file
      parent - parent section (or null if this is the root section)
      route - absolute (starting from the root file) route to this section
      previous - previous block at the same position, used to reference comments from
      mappings - raw (containing Java values directly; no Block instances) content map
      See Also:
      • superclass constructor used

    • Section

      protected Section(@NotNull Map<Object,Block<?>> defaultMap)
      Creates a section using the given (not necessarily empty) instance of default map.

      Sets the root file, parent section, name and route to null.

      This constructor is only used by the extending class, where nodes are unknown at the time of initialization. It is needed to call init(YamlDocument, Node, MappingNode, ExtendedConstructor) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      defaultMap - the content map
      See Also:
      • superclass constructor used

  • Method Details

    • initEmpty

      protected void initEmpty(@NotNull YamlDocument root)
      Initializes this section as an empty one.

      This method must only be called if isRoot() returns true. Expect an IllegalStateException otherwise.

      Parameters:
      root - the root file

    • init

      protected void init(@NotNull YamlDocument root, @Nullable org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.Node keyNode, @NotNull org.snakeyaml.engine.v2.nodes.MappingNode valueNode, @NotNull ExtendedConstructor constructor)
      Initializes this section, and it's contents using the given parameters, while also initializing the superclass by calling Block.init(Node, Node).
      Parameters:
      root - the root file of this section
      keyNode - node which represents the key to this section, used only to retrieve comments (will throw an IllegalArgumentException if provided and this is the root)
      valueNode - node which represents this section's contents
      constructor - constructor used to construct all the nodes contained within the root file, used to retrieve Java instances of the nodes

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty(boolean deep)
      Returns true if this section is empty, false otherwise. The parameter indicates if to search subsections too, which gives the true indication if the section is empty.

      If deep is false, returns only the result of Map.isEmpty() called on the content map represented by this section. However, the section (underlying map) might also contain sections, which are empty, resulting in incorrect returned value by this method false.

      If deep == true, iterates through contents of this section. If any of the values is not a section, returns false. If it is a section, runs this method recursively (with deep set to true) and returns false if the result of that call is false. If the iteration finished and none of the sub-calls returned otherwise, returns true.

      Parameters:
      deep - if to search deeply
      Returns:
      whether this section is empty

    • isSection

      public boolean isSection()
      Description copied from class: Block
      Returns if this block represents a section.
      Specified by:
      isSection in class Block<Map<Object,Block<?>>>

      if this block represents a section

    • isRoot

      public boolean isRoot()
      Returns whether this section is simultaneously the root section (file).

      if this section is the root section

    • getRoot

      @NotNull public YamlDocument getRoot()
      Returns the root file of this section.
      Returns:
      the root file

    • getParent

      public Section getParent()
      Returns the parent section, or null if this section has no parent - the section is also the root (check isRoot()).
      Returns:
      the parent section, or null if none

    • getName

      @Nullable public Object getName()
      Returns the name of this section. Name is considered to be the key used to access this section from the parent section.

      If this section is the root (check isRoot()), returns null.

      Incompatible with Spigot/BungeeCord API, where those, if this section represented the root file, would return an empty string.

      Returns:
      the name of this section

    • getNameAsString

      @Nullable public String getNameAsString()
      Returns the name of this section as a string. Name is considered to be the key used to access this section from the parent section.

      If this section is the root (check isRoot()), returns null.

      Incompatible with Spigot/BungeeCord API, where those, if this section represented the root file, would return an empty string.

      Returns:
      the name of this section

    • getNameAsRoute

      @NotNull public Route getNameAsRoute()
      Returns the name of this section as a single-key route. Name is considered to be the key used to access this section from the parent section.

      If this section is the root (check isRoot()), returns null.

      Incompatible with Spigot/BungeeCord API, where those, if this section represented the root file, would return an empty string.

      Returns:
      the name of this section

    • getRoute

      @Nullable public Route getRoute()
      Returns the absolute route of this section (route to this section from the root getRoot()). If this section represents the root file (check isRoot()), returns null.

      Incompatible with Spigot/BungeeCord API, where those, if this section represented the root file, would return an empty string.

      Returns:
      the name of this section
      See Also:
      • getRoot()

    • getRouteAsString

      @Nullable public String getRouteAsString()
      Returns the absolute route of this section (route to this section from the root getRoot()) as a string. If this section represents the root file (check isRoot()), returns null.

      Incompatible with Spigot/BungeeCord API, where those, if this section represented the root file, would return an empty string.

      Returns:
      the name of this section
      See Also:
      • getRoot()

    • getSubRoute

      @NotNull public Route getSubRoute(@NotNull Object key)
      Returns sub-route for the specified key derived from this section's route.

      More formally, calls Route.addTo(Route, Object).

      Parameters:
      key - key to add
      Returns:
      sub-route derived from the section's absolute route
      See Also:
      • Route.addTo(Route, Object)

    • getDefaults

      @Nullable public Section getDefaults()
      Returns equivalent of this section in the defaults.

      If there is not such section, returns null.

      Returns:
      default equivalent, or null if there's not any
      See Also:
      • hasDefaults()

    • hasDefaults

      public boolean hasDefaults()
      Returns if this section has an equivalent in the defaults.
      Returns:
      if this section has an equivalent in the defaults

    • adaptKey

      @NotNull public Object adaptKey(@NotNull Object key)
      Adapts the given key, as defined by the key format currently in use (GeneralSettings.getKeyFormat()).

      More formally, if key format is STRING, returns the result of Object.toString() on the given key object, the key object given otherwise.

      Parameters:
      key - the key object to adapt
      Returns:
      the adapted key

    • getRoutes

      @NotNull public Set<Route> getRoutes(boolean deep)
      Returns set of routes in this section; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned set is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultSet(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, (effectively) returns the result of getKeys() with the keys converted to routes, each with one element - the key itself.

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete set of routes within this section.

      It is guaranteed that call to contains(Route) with any route from the returned set will return true (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain routes from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get routes deeply (from sub-sections)
      Returns:
      the complete set of routes

    • getRoutesAsStrings

      @NotNull public Set<String> getRoutesAsStrings(boolean deep)
      Returns set of routes (interpreted as strings) in this section; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned set is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultSet(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, (effectively) returns the result of getKeys() with the keys converted to string.

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete set of routes within this section.

      The returned routes will have the keys separated by the root's separator (GeneralSettings.getRouteSeparator()).

      It is guaranteed that call to contains(String) with any route from the returned set will return true (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain routes from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get routes deeply
      Returns:
      the complete set of string routes

    • getKeys

      @NotNull public Set<Object> getKeys()
      Returns set of direct keys (in this section only - not deep); while not keeping any reference to this section.

      More formally, returns the key set of the underlying map. The returned set is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultSet().

      The returned map will also contain keys from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Returns:
      the complete set of keys directly contained within this (only; not sub-) section
      See Also:
      • getRoutes(boolean)
      • getRoutesAsStrings(boolean)

    • getRouteMappedValues

      @NotNull public Map<Route,Object> getRouteMappedValues(boolean deep)
      Returns a complete map of route=value pairs; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned map is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultMap(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, returns (effectively) a copy of the underlying map with keys (which are stored as object instances) converted to routes each with one element - the key itself; with their corresponding values (might also be a section).

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete map of route=value pairs within this section.

      It is guaranteed that call to get(Route) with any route from the returned map will return the value assigned to that route in the returned map (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain entries from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get values from sub-sections too
      Returns:
      the complete map of route=value pairs

    • getStringRouteMappedValues

      @NotNull public Map<String,Object> getStringRouteMappedValues(boolean deep)
      Returns a complete map of route=value pairs; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned map is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultMap(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, returns (effectively) a copy of the underlying map with keys (which are stored as object instances) converted to string routes containing one key - the key itself; with their corresponding values (might also be a section).

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete map of route=value pairs within this section.

      The returned routes will have the keys separated by the root's separator (GeneralSettings.getRouteSeparator()).

      It is guaranteed that call to get(String) with any route from the returned map will return the value assigned to that route in the returned map (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain entries from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get values from sub-sections too
      Returns:
      the complete map of string route=value pairs, including sections

    • getRouteMappedBlocks

      @NotNull public Map<Route,Block<?>> getRouteMappedBlocks(boolean deep)
      Returns a complete map of route=block pairs; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned map is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultMap(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, returns (effectively) a copy of the underlying map with keys (which are stored as object instances) converted to routes each with one element - the key itself; with their corresponding blocks.

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete map of route=block pairs within this section.

      It is guaranteed that call to getBlock(Route) with any route from the returned map will return the block assigned to that route in the returned map (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain entries from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get blocks from sub-sections too
      Returns:
      the complete map of route=block pairs

    • getStringRouteMappedBlocks

      @NotNull public Map<String,Block<?>> getStringRouteMappedBlocks(boolean deep)
      Returns a complete map of route=block pairs; while not keeping any reference to this (or sub-) sections. The returned map is an instance of GeneralSettings.getDefaultMap(). The routes are relative to this section.

      If deep is set to false, returns (effectively) a copy of the underlying map with keys (which are stored as object instances) converted to string routes containing one key - the key itself; with their corresponding blocks.

      Otherwise, also iterates through all sub-sections, while returning truly complete map of route=block pairs within this section.

      The returned routes will have the keys separated by the root's separator (GeneralSettings.getRouteSeparator()).

      It is guaranteed that call to getBlock(String) with any route from the returned map will return the block assigned to that route in the returned map (unless modified in between).

      The returned map will also contain entries from the equivalent section in the defaults, if any. Disable use of defaults (#3).

      Parameters:
      deep - if to get blocks from sub-sections too
      Returns:
      the complete map of route=block pairs

    • contains

      public boolean contains(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns whether this section contains anything at the given route. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check
      Returns:
      if this section contains anything at the given route
      See Also:
      • getBlock(Route)

    • contains

      public boolean contains(@NotNull String route)
      Returns whether this section contains anything at the given route. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check
      Returns:
      if this section contains anything at the given route
      See Also:
      • getBlock(String)

    • createSection

      public Section createSection(@NotNull Route route)
      Creates sections along the whole route, including the route itself and returns the section created at the end (at the route given).

      If there is a section already at the route, nothing is overwritten and the already existing section is returned.

      Please note that anything in the way will be overwritten. However, comments of each already existing block along the route will be kept and will be assigned to the new section created at its place.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to create a section at (with all parent sections)
      Returns:
      the created section at the given route, or the already existing one

    • createSection

      public Section createSection(@NotNull String route)
      Creates sections along the whole route, including the route itself and returns the section created at the end (at the route given).

      If there is a section already at the route, nothing is overwritten and the already existing section is returned.

      Please note that anything in the way will be overwritten. However, comments of each already existing block along the route will be kept and will be assigned to the new section created at its place.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to create a section at (with all parent sections)
      Returns:
      the created section at the given route, or the already existing one

    • repopulate

      public void repopulate(@NotNull Map<Object,Block<?>> mappings)
      Repopulates (clears and sets) the section with all the given direct mappings into this section in order as returned by the given map's iterator.

      Direct mappings mean that the given mappings will be inserted directly into this map. Therefore, this implementation does not support routes as keys and will not be able to process them (use setAll(Map) instead). All the mappings can then be retrieved via Block.getStoredValue() exactly in form as they were given.

      The setting of the values including the limitations follows the documentation defined by set(Route, Object).

      Parameters:
      mappings - mappings to repopulate the section with
      See Also:
      • set(Route, Object)

    • setAll

      public void setAll(@NotNull Map<Route,Object> mappings)
      Sets all the given mappings into this section in order as returned by the given map's iterator.

      The setting of the values including the limitations follows the documentation defined by set(Route, Object).

      Parameters:
      mappings - mappings to set

    • set

      public void set(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable Object value)
      Sets the given value at the given route in this section - overwrites the already existing value (if any). If there are sections missing to the route where the object should be set, they are created along the way.

      As the value to set, you can give instances of anything, with the following warnings:

      • null: valid value (please use remove(Route) to remove entries),
      • Section: the given section will be moved here (including comments, it will be deleted from the previous location),
      • any other Block: the given block will be pasted here (including comments, !!will keep reference to the previous location, delete it manually from there!!),
      • Map: a section will be created and initialized by the contents of the given map and comments of the previous block at that key (if any); where the map must only contain raw content (e.g. no Block instances; please see Section(YamlDocument, Section, Route, Block, Map) for more information),

      If there's any entry at the given route, it's comments are kept and assigned to the new entry (does not apply when the value is an instance of Block, in which case comments from the block are preserved).

      Attempt to set an instance of Section whose call to isRoot() returns true is considered illegal and will result in an IllegalArgumentException. Similarly, attempting to move sections between two different files with different key formats will result in such exception.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to set at
      value - the value to set

    • set

      public void set(@NotNull String route, @Nullable Object value)
      Sets the given value at the given route in this section - overwrites the already existing value (if any). If there are sections missing to the route where the object should be set, they are created along the way.

      As the value to set, you can give instances of anything, with the following warnings:

      • null: valid value (please use remove(Route) to remove entries),
      • non-root Section: the given section will be moved here (including comments, it will be deleted from the previous location),
      • any other Block: the given block will be pasted here (including comments, !!will keep reference to the previous location, delete it manually from there!!),
      • Map: a section will be created and initialized by the contents of the given map and comments of the previous block at that key (if any); where the map must only contain raw content (e.g. no Block instances; please see Section(YamlDocument, Section, Route, Block, Map) for more information),

      If there's any entry at the given route, it's comments are kept and assigned to the new entry (does not apply when the value is an instance of Block, in which case comments from the block are preserved).

      Attempt to set an instance of Section whose call to isRoot() returns true is considered illegal and will result in an IllegalArgumentException. Similarly, attempting to move sections between two different files with different key formats will result in such exception.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to set at
      value - the value to set

    • move

      @Nullable public Block<?> move(@NotNull Route source, @NotNull Route destination)
      Moves a block (comments and value) from the source to the destination route and returns the moved instance, if any.

      Please note that moving does not mean cloning (or copying), as the moved content will no longer be present at its original location.

      Parameters:
      source - the source route
      destination - the destination route
      Returns:
      the moved block, or null if there is no content at the source route

    • move

      @Nullable public Block<?> move(@NotNull String source, @NotNull String destination)
      Moves a block (comments and value) from the source to the destination route and returns the moved instance, if any.

      Please note that moving does not mean cloning (or copying), as the moved content will no longer be present at its original location.

      Parameters:
      source - the source route
      destination - the destination route
      Returns:
      the moved block, or null if there is no content at the source route

    • remove

      public boolean remove(@NotNull Route route)
      Removes entry at the given route (if any); returns true if successfully removed. The method returns false if and only no entry exists at the route.
      Parameters:
      route - the route to remove the entry at
      Returns:
      if anything was removed

    • remove

      public boolean remove(@NotNull String route)
      Removes entry at the given route (if any); returns true if successfully removed. The method returns false if and only no entry exists at the route (nothing was removed).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to remove the entry at
      Returns:
      if anything was removed

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears content within this section.

    • getOptionalBlock

      public Optional<Block<?>> getOptionalBlock(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns block at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If there is no block present (no value) at the given route, returns an empty optional.

      Each value is encapsulated in a Block: Section or TerminatedBlock instances. See the wiki for more information.

      Functionality notes: When individual elements (keys) of the given route are traversed, they are (without modifying the route object given - it is immutable) adapted to the current key format setting (see adaptKey(Object)).

      This is one of the fundamental methods, upon which the functionality of other methods in this class is built.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the block at
      Returns:
      block at the given route encapsulated in an optional

    • getOptionalBlock

      public Optional<Block<?>> getOptionalBlock(@NotNull String route)
      Returns block at the given string route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If there is no block present (no value) at the given route, returns an empty optional.

      Each value is encapsulated in a Block: Section or TerminatedBlock instances. See the wiki for more information.

      Functionality notes: The given route must contain individual keys separated using the separator character configured using GeneralSettings.Builder.setRouteSeparator(char).

      If the given string route does not contain the separator character (is only one key), the route refers to content in this section.

      Otherwise, traverses appropriate subsections determined by the keys contained, and returns the block at the last key defined in the given route (just like paths to files...). For example, for route separator '.' and route a.b, this method firstly attempts to get the section at key "a" in this section, then block at "b" in that (keyed as "a") section.

      We can also interpret this behaviour as a call to getOptionalBlock(Route) with route created via constructor Route.fromString(String, char) (which effectively splits the given string route into separate string keys according to the separator).

      This method works independently of the root's GeneralSettings.getKeyFormat(). However, as the given route contains individual string keys, if set to GeneralSettings.KeyFormat.OBJECT, you will only be able to access data at routes containing only keys parsed as strings (no integer, boolean... or null keys) by SnakeYAML Engine. If such functionality is needed, use getOptionalBlock(Route) instead.

      Please note that compatibility with Spigot/BungeeCord API is not maintained regarding empty string keys, where those APIs would return the instance of the current block - this section.

      This is one of the fundamental methods, upon which the functionality of other methods in this class is built.

      Parameters:
      route - the string route to get the block at
      Returns:
      block at the given route encapsulated in an optional

    • getBlock

      public Block<?> getBlock(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns the block encapsulated in the result of getOptionalBlock(Route).

      If it's an empty Optional, returns nullor value from defaults (#1).

      Parameters:
      route - the string route to get the block at
      Returns:
      block at the given route, or null if it doesn't exist
      See Also:
      • getOptionalBlock(Route)

    • getBlock

      public Block<?> getBlock(@NotNull String route)
      Returns the block encapsulated in the result of getOptionalBlock(String).

      If it's an empty Optional, returns nullor value from defaults (#1).

      Parameters:
      route - the string route to get the block at
      Returns:
      block at the given route, or null if it doesn't exist
      See Also:
      • getOptionalBlock(String)

    • getParent

      public Optional<Section> getParent(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns section at the parent route of the given one, encapsulated in an instance of Optional. Said differently, the returned section is the result of getSection(Route) called for the same route as given here, with the last route element removed.

      That means, this method ignores if the given route represents an existing block. If block at that parent route is not a section, returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the parent section from
      Returns:
      section at the parent route from the given one encapsulated in an optional

    • getParent

      public Optional<Section> getParent(@NotNull String route)
      Returns section at the parent route of the given one, encapsulated in an instance of Optional. Said differently, the returned section is effectively the result of getSection(Route) called for the same route as given here, with the last route element removed (Route.parent()).

      That means, this method ignores if the given route represents an existing block. If block at that parent route is not a section, returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the parent section from
      Returns:
      section at the parent route from the given one encapsulated in an optional

    • getOptional

      public Optional<Object> getOptional(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance; encapsulated in an instance of Optional.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      Returns:
      the value, or section at the given route

    • getOptional

      public Optional<Object> getOptional(@NotNull String route)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance; encapsulated in an instance of Optional.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      Returns:
      the value, or section at the given route

    • get

      public Object get(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns default value defined by root's general settings GeneralSettings.getDefaultObject()or value from defaults.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      Returns:
      the value at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

    • get

      public Object get(@NotNull String route)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns default value defined by root's general settings GeneralSettings.getDefaultObject()or value from defaults.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      Returns:
      the value at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

    • get

      public Object get(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable Object def)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns the provided default.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the value at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

    • get

      public Object get(@NotNull String route, @Nullable Object def)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value - list, integer...) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), returns the provided default.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the value at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

    • getAsOptional

      public <T> Optional<T> getAsOptional(@NotNull Route route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance, in both cases cast to instance of the given class; encapsulated in an instance of Optional.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), or the value (block's actual value or Section instance) is not castable to the given type, returns an empty optional.

      This method supports casting between primitive types and their non-primitive representations (e.g. Double - double) and also between two different numeric types (e.g. Double - int).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      the value cast to the given type

    • getAsOptional

      public <T> Optional<T> getAsOptional(@NotNull String route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns the value of the block (the actual value) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance, in both cases cast to instance of the given class; encapsulated in an instance of Optional.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), or the value (block's actual value or Section instance) is not castable to the given type, returns an empty optional.

      This method supports casting between primitive types and their non-primitive representations (e.g. Double - double) and also between two different numeric types (e.g. Double - int).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      the value cast to the given type

      Returns the value of the block (the actual value) at the given route, or if it is a section, the corresponding Section instance, in both cases cast to instance of the given class.

      If there is no block present at the given route (therefore no value can be returned), or the value (block's actual value or Section instance) is not castable to the given type, returns the provided default.

    • is

      public <T> boolean is(@NotNull Route route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns true if and only value (list, integer... or Section) at the given route exists, and it is an instance of the given class. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).

      This method supports casting between primitive types and their non-primitive representations (e.g. Double - double).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      if a value exists at the given route, and it is an instance of the given class

    • is

      public <T> boolean is(@NotNull String route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns true if and only value (list, integer... or Section) at the given route exists, and it is an instance of the given class. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).

      This method supports casting between primitive types and their non-primitive representations (e.g. Double - double).

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      if a value exists at the given route, and it is an instance of the given class

    • getOptionalString

      public Optional<String> getOptionalString(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns string at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, String instance is preferred. Anything else is converted to one using Object.toString().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the string at
      Returns:
      the string at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(Route)

    • getOptionalString

      public Optional<String> getOptionalString(@NotNull String route)
      Returns string at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, String instance is preferred. Anything else is converted to one using Object.toString().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the string at
      Returns:
      the string at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(String)

    • getString

      public String getString(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns string at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultString()or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, String instance is preferred. Anything else is converted to one using Object.toString().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the string at
      Returns:
      the string at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getString(Route, String)

    • getOptionalEnum

      public <T extends Enum<T>> Optional<T> getOptionalEnum(@NotNull Route route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns enum at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, it is not an enum constant of T or cannot be converted to one (see below), returns an empty optional.

      If there is an object other than an enum whose string representation matches any of the constants defined by the T, the corresponding constant is returned.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target enum type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the enum at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      the enum constant at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(Route)

    • getOptionalEnum

      public <T extends Enum<T>> Optional<T> getOptionalEnum(@NotNull String route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns enum at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, it is not an enum constant of T or cannot be converted to one (see below), returns an empty optional.

      If there is an object other than an enum whose string representation matches any of the constants defined by the T, the corresponding constant is returned.

      Type Parameters:
      T - the target enum type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the enum at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      the enum constant at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(String)

    • getEnum

      public <T extends Enum<T>> T getEnum(@NotNull Route route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns enum at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, it is not an enum constant of T or cannot be converted to one (see below), returns null or value from defaults (#1).

      If there is an object other than an enum whose string representation matches any of the constants defined by the T, the corresponding constant is returned.

      Type Parameters:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • isEnum

      public <T extends Enum<T>> boolean isEnum(@NotNull Route route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an enum constant of T, or object other than an enum whose string representation matches any of the constants defined by T. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Type Parameters:
      T - the target enum type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is an instance, or convertible to an enum of the target type
      See Also:
      • get(Route)

    • isEnum

      public <T extends Enum<T>> boolean isEnum(@NotNull String route, @NotNull Class<T> clazz)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an enum constant of T, or object other than an enum whose string representation matches any of the constants defined by T. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Type Parameters:
      T - the target enum type
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      clazz - class of the target type
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a constant of, or convertible to an enum of the target type
      See Also:
      • get(String)

    • getOptionalChar

      public Optional<Character> getOptionalChar(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns char at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      Returns:
      the char at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(Route)

    • getOptionalChar

      public Optional<Character> getOptionalChar(@NotNull String route)
      Returns char at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      Returns:
      the char at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptional(String)

    • getChar

      public Character getChar(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns char at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultChar()or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      Returns:
      the char at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getChar(Route, Character)

    • getChar

      public Character getChar(@NotNull String route)
      Returns char at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultChar()or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      Returns:
      the char at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getChar(String, Character)

    • getChar

      public Character getChar(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable Character def)
      Returns char at the given route.

      If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the char at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalChar(Route)

    • getChar

      public Character getChar(@NotNull String route, @Nullable Character def)
      Returns char at the given route.

      If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, Character instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of String and it is exactly 1 character in length, returns that character. If is an Integer (or primitive variant), it is converted to a character (by casting, see the ASCII table).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the char at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the char at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalChar(String)

    • isChar

      public boolean isChar(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a Character, or any other compatible type defined by getOptionalChar(Route). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a character, or any other compatible type
      See Also:
      • get(Route)

    • isChar

      public boolean isChar(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a Character, or any other compatible type defined by getOptionalChar(String). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a character, or any other compatible type
      See Also:
      • get(String)

    • getOptionalNumber

      public Optional<Number> getOptionalNumber(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns number at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns an empty optional.
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the number at
      Returns:
      the number at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalNumber

      public Optional<Number> getOptionalNumber(@NotNull String route)
      Returns number at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns an empty optional.
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the number at
      Returns:
      the number at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(String, Class)

    • getNumber

      public Number getNumber(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns number at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber(), or value from defaults (#1).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the number at
      Returns:
      the number at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getNumber(Route, Number)

    • getNumber

      public Number getNumber(@NotNull String route)
      Returns number at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber(), or value from defaults (#1).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the number at
      Returns:
      the number at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getNumber(String, Number)

    • getNumber

      public Number getNumber(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable Number def)
      Returns number at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns the provided default.
      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the number at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the number at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

      public Number getNumber(@NotNull String route, @Nullable Number def)
      Returns number at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a Number, returns the provided default.
      Parameters:

      the number at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:

    • isNumber

      public boolean isNumber(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an Number. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a number
      See Also:
      • get(Route)

    • isNumber

      public boolean isNumber(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an Number. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a number
      See Also:
      • get(String)

    • getOptionalInt

      public Optional<Integer> getOptionalInt(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns number at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalInt

      public Optional<Integer> getOptionalInt(@NotNull String route)
      Returns integer at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(String, Class)

    • getInt

      public Integer getInt(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to Integer as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getInt(Route, Integer)

    • getInt

      public Integer getInt(@NotNull String route)
      Returns integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to Integer as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getInt(String, Integer)

    • getInt

      public Integer getInt(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable Integer def)
      Returns integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalInt(Route)

    • getInt

      public Integer getInt(@NotNull String route, @Nullable Integer def)
      Returns integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, Integer instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.intValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the integer at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the integer at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalInt(Route)

    • isInt

      public boolean isInt(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an Integer (or the primitive variant). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is an integer
      See Also:
      • get(Route)

    • isInt

      public boolean isInt(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is an Integer (or the primitive variant). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is an integer
      See Also:
      • get(String)

    • getOptionalBigInt

      public Optional<BigInteger> getOptionalBigInt(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns big integer at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalBigInt

      public Optional<BigInteger> getOptionalBigInt(@NotNull String route)
      Returns big integer at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getBigInt

      public BigInteger getBigInt(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns big integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to BigInteger as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getBigInt(Route, BigInteger)

    • getBigInt

      public BigInteger getBigInt(@NotNull String route)
      Returns big integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to BigInteger as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getBigInt(Route, BigInteger)

    • getBigInt

      public BigInteger getBigInt(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable BigInteger def)
      Returns big integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptionalBigInt(Route)

      public BigInteger getBigInt(@NotNull String route, @Nullable BigInteger def)
      Returns big integer at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns the provided default.

      Natively, BigInteger instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is big integer created from the result of Number.longValue() using BigInteger.valueOf(long).

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the big integer at the given route
      See Also:

      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a BigInteger. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).

    • isBoolean

      public boolean isBoolean(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a Boolean (or the primitive variant). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).

    • getOptionalDouble

      public Optional<Double> getOptionalDouble(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns double at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Double instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.doubleValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the double at
      Returns:
      the double at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalDouble

      public Optional<Double> getOptionalDouble(@NotNull String route)
      Returns double at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Double instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.doubleValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the double at
      Returns:
      the double at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(String, Class)

    • getDouble

      public Double getDouble(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns double at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to Double as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Double instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.doubleValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the double at
      Returns:
      the double at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getDouble(Route, Double)

    • getOptionalFloat

      public Optional<Float> getOptionalFloat(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns float at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Float instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.floatValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the float at
      Returns:
      the float at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalFloat

      public Optional<Float> getOptionalFloat(@NotNull String route)
      Returns float at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Float instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.floatValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the float at
      Returns:
      the float at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getFloat

      public Float getFloat(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns float at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to Float as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Float instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.floatValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the float at
      Returns:
      the float at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

      Natively, Float instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.floatValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the float at
      Returns:
      the float at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

      See Also:
      • get(Route)

    • isFloat

      public boolean isFloat(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a Float (or the primitive variant). If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).
      Parameters:
      route - the route to check the value at
      Returns:
      if a value at the given route exists and is a float
      See Also:
      • get(String)

    • getOptionalByte

      public Optional<Byte> getOptionalByte(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns byte at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Byte instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.byteValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the byte at
      Returns:
      the byte at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(Route, Class)

    • getOptionalByte

      public Optional<Byte> getOptionalByte(@NotNull String route)
      Returns byte at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns an empty optional.

      Natively, Byte instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.byteValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the byte at
      Returns:
      the byte at the given route
      See Also:
      • getAsOptional(String, Class)

    • getByte

      public Byte getByte(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns byte at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not an instance of any compatible type (see below), returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultNumber() (converted to Byte as defined below), or value from defaults (#1).

      Natively, Byte instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.byteValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the byte at
      Returns:
      the byte at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getByte(Route, Byte)

      Natively, Byte instance is preferred. However, if there is an instance of Number, the value returned is the result of Number.byteValue().

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the byte at
      Returns:
      the byte at the given route, or default according to the documentation above

    • isList

      public boolean isList(@NotNull String route)
      Returns true if and only a value at the given route exists, and it is a List. If no value is at the route, checks the defaults (#2).

    • getOptionalStringList

      public Optional<List<String>> getOptionalStringList(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns list of strings at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns an empty optional.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not compatible as documented at getOptionalString(Route), it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the string list at
      Returns:
      the string list at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptionalList(Route)
      • getOptionalString(Route)

    • getOptionalStringList

      public Optional<List<String>> getOptionalStringList(@NotNull String route)
      Returns list of strings at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns an empty optional.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not compatible as documented at getOptionalString(String), it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the string list at
      Returns:
      the string list at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptionalList(String)
      • getOptionalString(String)

      public List<BigInteger> getBigIntList(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable List<BigInteger> def)
      Returns list of big integers at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns the provided default.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not compatible as documented at getOptionalBigInt(Route), it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the big integer list at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the big integer list at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalBigIntList(Route)
      • getOptionalBigInt(Route)

    • getBigIntList

    • getOptionalMapList

      public Optional<List<Map<?,?>>> getOptionalMapList(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns list of maps at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns an empty optional.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section. It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(Route, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptionalList(Route)

    • getOptionalMapList

      public Optional<List<Map<?,?>>> getOptionalMapList(@NotNull String route)
      Returns list of maps at the given route encapsulated in an instance of Optional. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns an empty optional.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section. It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(String, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route
      See Also:
      • getOptionalList(String)

    • getMapList

      public List<Map<?,?>> getMapList(@NotNull Route route, @Nullable List<Map<?,?>> def)
      Returns list of maps at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns the provided default.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section (unless the default value is returned). It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(Route, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalMapList(Route)

    • getMapList

      public List<Map<?,?>> getMapList(@NotNull String route, @Nullable List<Map<?,?>> def)
      Returns list of maps at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns the provided default.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section (unless the default value is returned). It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(String, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      def - the default value
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getOptionalMapList(String)

    • getMapList

      public List<Map<?,?>> getMapList(@NotNull Route route)
      Returns list of maps at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList()or value from defaults.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section (unless the default value is returned). It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(Route, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
      See Also:
      • getMapList(Route, List)

    • getMapList

      public List<Map<?,?>> getMapList(@NotNull String route)
      Returns list of maps at the given route. If nothing is present at the given route, or is not a List, returns default defined by root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList()or value from defaults.

      This method creates and returns a new instance of root's GeneralSettings.getDefaultList(), with the elements re-added (to the target/returned list) from the (source) list at the given route one by one, in order determined by the list iterator. If any of the elements of the source list is not an instance of Map, it is skipped and will not appear in the returned list.

      Please note that this method does not clone the maps returned - mutating them affects the list stored in the section (unless the default value is returned). It is, however, if needed, still recommended to call set(String, Object) afterwards.

      Parameters:
      route - the route to get the map list at
      Returns:
      the map list at the given route, or default according to the documentation above
